services.summary: Residential local moving for homes, condos, townhomes, apartments, and small offices across Metro Vancouver and the Lower Mainland.

How long does a cross-Canada move take?

pricing.name: Local moving pricing
pricing.answer: Local moving pricing in Metro Vancouver depends on crew size, time required, access, stairs or elevator bookings, packing needs, travel time, and specialty items. Purely Canadian Movers provides a written estimate before booking so customers can understand the expected cost before moving day.
pricing.priceModel: Quoted based on move details. Local moves commonly use crew size and time as major pricing drivers.

pricing.name: Long-distance moving pricing
pricing.answer: Long-distance moving costs in Canada depend on shipment weight or volume, route distance, pickup and delivery access, timing, packing, storage, specialty handling, and valuation coverage. A written estimate is the right way to confirm the final cost.
pricing.priceModel: Quoted based on inventory, route, timing, and service requirements.

pricing.name: Packing service pricing
pricing.answer: Packing service pricing depends on the number of rooms, box count, fragile items, packing materials, and whether the customer needs full packing, partial packing, or unpacking help.
pricing.priceModel: Quoted based on packing scope and material needs.

pricing.name: Storage pricing
pricing.answer: Storage pricing depends on the volume of goods, storage duration, pickup and delivery requirements, and whether storage is paired with a local or long-distance move.
pricing.priceModel: Quoted based on volume, duration, and handling requirements.

faqs.question: How far in advance should I book movers?
faqs.answer: Book as early as possible, especially for month-end, summer, weekends, and long-distance moves. A few weeks of notice gives the team better scheduling options and more time to prepare an accurate estimate.

faqs.question: How much do movers cost in Metro Vancouver?
faqs.answer: Moving cost depends on crew size, time required, travel time, access, stairs, elevators, packing, and specialty items. The best next step is a written estimate based on the actual move details.

faqs.question: Do movers charge travel time?
faqs.answer: Travel time can be part of moving pricing depending on the move type, route, and estimate details. Customers should ask what is included in the written estimate before booking.

faqs.question: Do I need to reserve the elevator?
faqs.answer: For condos and apartments, customers should reserve the elevator and confirm loading zone rules before moving day. Elevator delays can increase move time and make scheduling harder.

faqs.question: Do you use subcontractors?
faqs.answer: Purely Canadian Movers emphasizes direct accountability and no subcontractors for local moves. Long-distance moves may involve coordinated van line network support through Great Canadian Van Lines.

faqs.question: Can movers pack fragile items?
faqs.answer: Yes. Packing support can include fragile items, kitchens, artwork, glass, electronics, and full or partial home packing depending on the customer's needs.

faqs.question: Can storage be included with my move?
faqs.answer: Yes. Storage can be useful when possession dates do not line up, renovations are underway, or a long-distance move needs staged pickup or delivery.

faqs.question: Are belongings protected during the move?
faqs.answer: Valuation coverage options should be reviewed before moving day. Customers with high-value, fragile, or sentimental items should discuss protection options during the estimate process.

policies.name: Estimate and booking policy
policies.summary: Customers should request a written estimate and confirm move date, inventory, access, services, and timing before booking.
publicNotes: Written estimates should reflect inventory and access details.
Month-end, summer, and weekend moves should be booked earlier when possible.
Changes to inventory, access, packing, or route may change the final estimate.

policies.name: Valuation and coverage
policies.summary: Customers should review valuation coverage options before moving day, especially for long-distance moves and high-value goods.
publicNotes: Basic carrier liability and additional valuation options are not the same as separate home insurance.
Customers should disclose high-value or fragile items during the estimate process.
Damage concerns should be documented promptly with photos and item details.

policies.name: Building access and elevator planning
policies.summary: Condo, apartment, office, and high-rise moves need elevator booking, loading zone planning, and building access confirmation.
publicNotes: Customers should reserve elevators before moving day.
Loading zone and parking restrictions can affect timing.
Building rules should be shared with the moving team before arrival.
